title: Post-push GitHub CI watch + release CI verification — remote ubuntu CI is the only Linux gate and it is currently fire-and-forget; main stayed red for a full day unnoticed by the loop
summary: Local gates run on macOS only; the go.yml matrix runs ubuntu+macos, so ubuntu-only failures (e.g. TestOrchestrator_TriageLeakRecover: git identity auto-detect differs per OS) are STRUCTURALLY invisible to every local gate. Today nothing in the loop or the release pipeline reads the outcome of the CI run a push triggers: main was red from 2026-07-06 11:50 through 20:16+ across 8 pushes and the loop kept shipping on top (release-hardening memory: v22.0.0 was cut on red CI for the same reason).
root_cause: The pipeline treats 'pushed' as 'done' — no feedback edge from GitHub Actions back into the loop's signal set, and release preflight verifies binaries but never the remote CI verdict on the release commit.
fix: Deterministic Go, config-driven: (1) post-ship (or supervisor batch-end) CI watch — after a push, poll the GitHub run for the pushed SHA (gh run list --commit <sha> / REST equivalent via existing gh auth) with a policy-configured timeout+interval; record the verdict in the cycle dossier; on failure, auto-file a critical fix-forward inbox item carrying the failing job/test extract (bounded log excerpt). (2) Release preflight (releasepreflight pkg) gains a hard check: the release commit's go workflow run must be conclusion=success before tagging (override only via explicit operator flag). (3) All knobs (enabled, timeout, poll interval) in policy.json — no env flags.
acceptance: A push whose CI run fails yields a critical inbox item naming the failing test within one batch boundary (integration test with a faked gh runner).
evolve release refuses to tag when the release commit's go CI run is not green (test with faked run state); explicit override flag exists and logs loudly.
CI verdict appears in the cycle dossier.
Zero new env flags; knobs live in policy.json.
